page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc9i.6xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor operating at a base frequency of 3.4 GHz. The instance provides 24 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core configuration, ensuring that workloads have exclusive access to CPU resources without hyperthreading overhead.

This instance is configured with 48.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage, requiring network-attached storage options for persistent data. Network connectivity is supported by an 18 Gbps baseline bandwidth, facilitating efficient data transfer rates for network-bound operations.

Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated physical cores, the ecs.hfc9i.6xlarge offers a highly focused compute profile. This design is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy tasks that do not demand large memory footprints or local storage. It is suitable for workloads such as high-performance computing, batch processing, compute-intensive web serving, and scientific modeling.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc9i.6xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 24 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor running at 3.4 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture on a KVM hypervisor, this server provides a single thread per core configuration to eliminate hyperthreading resource contention. It is equipped with 48.0 GB of RAM, representing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and contains no local storage, relying instead on network-attached storage. Network performance is supported by an 18 Gbps baseline bandwidth. This hardware configuration is designed for compute-intensive workloads such as batch processing, scientific modeling, and high-performance web serving where dedicated CPU performance is prioritized over large memory allocations or local disk space.
